description: Precision-demo drivers: one executable per target function. Orchestration tools should read this file; CMake lists DEMO_DRIVERS in CMakeLists.txt (keep in sync when adding entries).

positional_arguments.name: batch_size
positional_arguments.description: Number of random inputs; Kokkos parallel_for range length.

positional_arguments.name: output_file
positional_arguments.description: Path to write results (format defined by each driver; ddilog uses hex bit patterns per sample).

positional_arguments.name: seed
positional_arguments.description: uint64 seed for the RNG; fixed seed gives a reproducible baseline.

numerical_contract_defaults.notes: Override per driver when needed. Used by future compare / agent loops.

input_domain_conventions.description: Each driver may define input_domain.inputs[]: name, semantic, distribution (e.g. uniform_real), closed_interval [min,max] where applicable, dtype, plus implementation path. This is the catalog of record; keep driver source aligned until CLI overrides exist.

drivers.summary: Batch evaluation of ql::ddilog on random real scalars (double template parameters).

input_domain.implementation: driver/ddilog_driver.cc (std::uniform_real_distribution; must match closed_interval until the driver accepts min/max on the CLI).

mutation_candidates.description: Stack locals inside ql::ddilog in kokkosUtils.h for mixed-precision trials (e.g. downcast one at a time vs double baseline).
